title: Trust, Hope, and Promise as Pre-Commitment Operators
one_sentence: This module defines trust, hope, and promise as pre-commitment operators that insert unobserved future transitions into present evaluation.
dependency_sequence: ethics validates real vs phantom shared attractors
pre-commitment keeps relational persistence open under uncertainty

trust_vs_belief.trust: Other / relation; opens relational path; risk = betrayal
trust_vs_belief.belief: World / model; stabilizes world hypothesis; risk = illusion

negative_constraints: Do not treat trust as belief.
Do not treat hope as optimism.
Do not treat promise as unilateral intention.
Do not treat this module as religious faith, legal contract doctrine, or romantic advice.
